name: Avalokiteśvara Practice (Great Compassion Dhāraṇī Sādhana)
name_chinese: 觀音菩薩 — Guānyīn Púsà (Thousand-Armed, Thousand-Eyed)
primary_purpose: Awakening Great Compassion; healing; protection from all dangers for self and all beings

mantras.use: Primary Great Compassion Dhāraṇī (Da Bei Zhou)
Six-syllable mantra — Oṃ Maṇi Padme Hūṃ

visualizations: Thousand-Armed, Thousand-Eyed Avalokiteśvara appears in the sky before you, standing on a lotus
His/Her body is brilliant white, suffused with hues of green and soft pink
Eleven heads crown the body — the 10 stages of bodhisattva development plus the head of Amitabha at the top
One thousand arms radiate outward like a mandala; each palm contains an eye of wisdom — seeing all suffering and reaching out to relieve it
The two central hands are pressed together at the heart in añjali mudrā (holding a wish-fulfilling gem)
Other principal hands hold: a white lotus (purity), a crystal vase (healing nectar), a willow branch (healing), a mala (recitation), a bow and arrow (skillful means), a wheel (dharma)
From his/her heart, white healing light radiates outward, dissolving the suffering of all beings — disease, danger, negative karma, and fear
Willow branch nectar sprinkles all beings, healing physical and emotional wounds
Visualize all beings — humans, animals, insects, beings in all realms — gathered around you, each receiving Avalokiteśvara's compassionate blessing
benefits: Healing of all physical and mental illnesses
Protection from 15 kinds of untimely death and 84,000 afflictions
Awakening of bodhicitta (the mind of enlightenment) in the practitioner
Rebirth in Sukhāvatī (Pure Land) at death — Avalokiteśvara is Amitabha's attendant
Liberation of all beings from suffering through one's compassion
Developing the universal compassion that is the essence of all Mahayana practice
frequency_purpose: Solfeggio frequency of connection and relationships — resonates with Avalokiteśvara's universal compassion
element: Wind (movement of compassion to all places)
vow: Avalokiteśvara vowed: 'Should any being hear my name, see my form, or recite my mantra, may they be freed from all suffering. Should I ever waver in compassion, may my head split into a thousand pieces.' The vow was sealed — thousand arms and thousand eyes manifested.
fifteen_protections: Protection from poisons and contagious diseases
Protection from wild beasts and snakes
Protection from lightning, fire, and drowning
Protection from falls from cliffs or heights
Protection from evil spirits and demonic influence
Protection from being cursed or bewitched
Protection from enemy armies and war
Protection from sudden quarrels and disputes
Protection from untimely winds and storms
Protection from being lost or wandering
Protection from mental disturbance and madness
best_times: Daily — traditionally recited 108 times each morning
On Guanyin's feast days (19th day of 2nd lunar month — birthday; 19th of 6th — enlightenment; 19th of 9th — ordination)
On the full moon (15th lunar day)
When healing is needed for self or others
When protection from danger is needed
During times of collective suffering or disaster
dedication: By the merit of this Great Compassion Dhāraṇī, may all beings throughout space be freed from suffering and the causes of suffering. May I become like Avalokiteśvara, working ceaselessly for the liberation of all.
